---
title: "Biomass"
author: "Zulema Carracedo Lorenzo"
date: "2025-01-31"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#data management
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(readxl)

#statistics
library(multcompView)
library(psych) #for summary table
library(DHARMa)
library(emmeans)
library(glmmTMB) # for generalized mixed models
library(car) # for Anova


#plots
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpattern)
library(Hmisc)
library(fitdistrplus)

# packages needed for Cohen's D
library(effsize)  
library(effectsize)
library(lsr)

#for tables
library(flextable)
library(officer)
library(car)
library(emmeans)
library(multcomp)

```

#Load data
```{r}

#Import data

biomass <- readxl::read_xlsx("./4_Arabidopsis_mutants_exp/data/raw/Biomass_raw.xlsx")
biomass <- as.data.frame(biomass)

metadata <- readxl::read_xlsx("./4_Arabidopsis_mutants_exp/data/raw/metadata.xlsx")
metadata <- as.data.frame(metadata)

biomass <- merge(metadata, biomass, by = "plate_id")

biomass[,1:4] <- lapply(biomass[,1:4], as.factor)
str(biomass)
```

#Arrange data 

```{r}

biomass$media <- factor(biomass$media, levels=c("control", "sorbitol"))
biomass$treatment <- factor(biomass$treatment, levels=c("mock", "PsWCS417"))
biomass$genotype <- factor(biomass$genotype, levels=c("col0", "bcat4", "snrk2.2/2.3", "sweet11/12"))

```

#Add new colums

```{r}

biomass <- biomass %>% mutate(shoot_biomass_seedling_mg = shoot_biomass_g/n_seedlings*1000)

biomass <- biomass %>% mutate(total_biomass_seedling_mg = total_biomass_g/n_seedlings*1000)

biomass <- biomass %>% mutate(root_biomass_seedling_mg = (total_biomass_g - shoot_biomass_g)/n_seedlings*1000)

biomass <- biomass %>% mutate(root_shoot_ratio = root_biomass_seedling_mg/shoot_biomass_seedling_mg)


summary(biomass)  

```


#Plots

```{r}


#Total fresh biomass

ggplot(biomass, aes(x = media, y = total_biomass_seedling_mg, fill = genotype, pattern = treatment)) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "genotype", values = c("#5AB4E5", "#00AEB2", "#F0E442", "#CC79A7")) +
  geom_boxplot_pattern( position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),
                        color = "black", 
                        pattern_fill = "black", 
                        pattern_angle = 45, 
                        pattern_density = 0.05, 
                        pattern_spacing = 0.01,
                        pattern_key_scale_factor = 0.6,
                        outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_pattern_manual(values = c(PsWCS417 = "stripe", mock = "none")) +
  guides(pattern = guide_legend(override.aes = list(fill = "white")), fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(pattern = "none"))) +
  ylab("Total FW (mg)") +
  geom_point(position = position_jitterdodge(), alpha=0.5, size = 2) + 
  theme_bw(18) + 
  theme(legend.position="top") +
  labs(fill = "media", pattern = " ") +
  guides(fill= FALSE) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank()) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +  # Force y-axis to start at 0
  facet_wrap(~genotype, nrow=1)

#save it
mapply(function(x)
  ggsave(
  plot = last_plot(),
  filename =x,
  path = "./4_Arabidopsis_mutants_exp/results/plots",
  scale = 1.8,
  width = 150,
  height = 100,
  units = "mm",
  dpi = 300),
x=c("Fig.S3A_Total_FW.svg","Fig.S3A_Total_FW.png"))



```


#Calculating effect size

```{r}
# Cohen's d

# per genotype 4 df

# For col-0
data_C_Ps_col0 <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(treatment == "PsWCS417") %>%
  filter(genotype == "col0")

data_C_mock_col0 <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(treatment == "mock") %>%
  filter(genotype == "col0")

data_S_Ps_col0 <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(treatment == "PsWCS417") %>%
  filter(genotype == "col0")

data_S_mock_col0 <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(treatment == "mock") %>%
  filter(genotype == "col0")

#Now calculate Cohen's D with CI with effect size package 


#for total FW

Cohens_total_col0_C <- cohens_d(data_C_Ps_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, data_C_mock_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, ci = 0.95)
Cohens_total_col0_C <- as.data.frame(Cohens_total_col0_C)
Cohens_total_col0_C <- Cohens_total_col0_C %>% mutate(genotype = "col0" ) %>% mutate(media = "control")

Cohens_total_col0_S <- cohens_d(data_S_Ps_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, data_S_mock_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, ci = 0.95)
Cohens_total_col0_S <- as.data.frame(Cohens_total_col0_S)
Cohens_total_col0_S <- Cohens_total_col0_S %>% mutate(genotype = "col0" ) %>% mutate(media = "sorbitol")


# For bcat4
data_C_Ps_bcat <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(treatment == "PsWCS417") %>%
  filter(genotype == "bcat4")

data_C_mock_bcat <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(treatment == "mock") %>%
  filter(genotype == "bcat4")

data_S_Ps_bcat <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(treatment == "PsWCS417") %>%
  filter(genotype == "bcat4")

data_S_mock_bcat <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(treatment == "mock") %>%
  filter(genotype == "bcat4")

#Now canculate Cohen's D with CI with effectsize package 

#for total FW

Cohens_total_bcat_C <- cohens_d(data_C_Ps_bcat$total_biomass_seedling_mg, data_C_mock_bcat$total_biomass_seedling_mg, ci = 0.95)
Cohens_total_bcat_C <- as.data.frame(Cohens_total_bcat_C)
Cohens_total_bcat_C <- Cohens_total_bcat_C %>% mutate(genotype = "bcat4" ) %>% mutate(media = "control")

Cohens_total_bcat_S <- cohens_d(data_S_Ps_bcat$total_biomass_seedling_mg, data_S_mock_bcat$total_biomass_seedling_mg, ci = 0.95)
Cohens_total_bcat_S <- as.data.frame(Cohens_total_bcat_S)
Cohens_total_bcat_S <- Cohens_total_bcat_S %>% mutate(genotype = "bcat4" ) %>% mutate(media = "sorbitol")


# For snrk2.2/2.3
data_C_Ps_snrk <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(treatment == "PsWCS417") %>%
  filter(genotype == "snrk2.2/2.3")

data_C_mock_snrk <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(treatment == "mock") %>%
  filter(genotype == "snrk2.2/2.3")

data_S_Ps_snrk <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(treatment == "PsWCS417") %>%
  filter(genotype == "snrk2.2/2.3")

data_S_mock_snrk <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(treatment == "mock") %>%
  filter(genotype == "snrk2.2/2.3")

#Now canculate Cohen's D with CI with effectsize package 

#for total FW

Cohens_total_snrk_C <- cohens_d(data_C_Ps_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, data_C_mock_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, ci = 0.95)
Cohens_total_snrk_C <- as.data.frame(Cohens_total_snrk_C)
Cohens_total_snrk_C <- Cohens_total_snrk_C %>% mutate(genotype = "snrk2.2/2.3" ) %>% mutate(media = "control")

Cohens_total_snrk_S <- cohens_d(data_S_Ps_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, data_S_mock_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, ci = 0.95)
Cohens_total_snrk_S <- as.data.frame(Cohens_total_snrk_S)
Cohens_total_snrk_S <- Cohens_total_snrk_S %>% mutate(genotype = "snrk2.2/2.3" ) %>% mutate(media = "sorbitol")


# For sweet11/12
data_C_Ps_sweet <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(treatment == "PsWCS417") %>%
  filter(genotype == "sweet11/12")

data_C_mock_sweet <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(treatment == "mock") %>%
  filter(genotype == "sweet11/12")

data_S_Ps_sweet <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(treatment == "PsWCS417") %>%
  filter(genotype == "sweet11/12")

data_S_mock_sweet <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(treatment == "mock") %>%
  filter(genotype == "sweet11/12")

#Now canculate Cohen's D with CI with effectsize package 

#for total FW

Cohens_total_sweet_C <- cohens_d(data_C_Ps_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, data_C_mock_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, ci = 0.95)
Cohens_total_sweet_C <- as.data.frame(Cohens_total_sweet_C)
Cohens_total_sweet_C <- Cohens_total_sweet_C %>% mutate(genotype = "sweet11/12" ) %>% mutate(media = "control")

Cohens_total_sweet_S <- cohens_d(data_S_Ps_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, data_S_mock_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, ci = 0.95)
Cohens_total_sweet_S <- as.data.frame(Cohens_total_sweet_S)
Cohens_total_sweet_S <- Cohens_total_sweet_S %>% mutate(genotype = "sweet11/12" ) %>% mutate(media = "sorbitol")


# make a df with all the Cohen's D results

cohens_total_df <- rbind(Cohens_total_col0_C, Cohens_total_col0_S, Cohens_total_bcat_C, Cohens_total_bcat_S, Cohens_total_snrk_C, Cohens_total_snrk_S, Cohens_total_sweet_C, Cohens_total_sweet_S)


#plot


#Total FW 

#arrange data first
cohens_total_df$media <- factor(cohens_total_df$media, levels=c("sorbitol","control"))
cohens_total_df$genotype <- factor(cohens_total_df$genotype, levels=c("sweet11/12", "snrk2.2/2.3", "bcat4", "col0"))

#Total biomass

ggplot(cohens_total_df, aes(x=genotype, y=Cohens_d, ymin=CI_low, ymax=CI_high, col=genotype,fill=genotype, shape = media)) + 
  scale_fill_manual(name = "genotype", values = c("#5AB4E5", "#00AEB2", "#F0E442", "#CC79A7")) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "genotype", values = c("#5AB4E5", "#00AEB2", "#F0E442", "#CC79A7")) +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0), lty=2, linewidth = 1, colour= "red") +
      geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(0.3), size = 1, linewidth = 1.5) +
      ggtitle("Total biomass") +
      xlab("Genotype") +
      scale_y_continuous(name="Cohen's D estimate with 95% confidence interval", limits = c(-1,6)) +
      coord_flip() +
      theme_bw(18) 

mapply(function(x)
  ggsave(
  plot = last_plot(),
  filename =x,
  path = "./4_Arabidopsis_mutants_exp/results/plots",
  scale = 1.8,
  width = 150,
  height = 100,
  units = "mm",
  dpi = 300),
x=c("Fig.3_Cohens_total_biomass.svg","Fig.3_Cohens_total_biomass.png"))



```

#Testing GLM from Karen

```{r}
#What if I do statistics separatly for Ms or MS+sorbitol

data_control <- subset(biomass, media == "control")

data_sorbitol <- subset(biomass, media == "sorbitol")

# Data distribution

aovx <- aov(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg ~ data_control$treatment + data_control$genotype)
shapiro.test(aovx$residuals)
# normaal

hist(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg)

boxplot(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg)

# Visualize data
plotdist(log(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg))

# Test the different distributions
normal <- fitdist(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg, "norm")
summary(normal)

gamma <- fitdist(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg, "gamma") 
summary(gamma)

poisson <- fitdist(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg, "pois")
summary(poisson)

exponential <- fitdist(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg, "exp")
summary(exponential)

nonbinom <- fitdist(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg, "nbinom")
summary(nonbinom)

lnormal <- fitdist(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg, "lnorm")
summary(lnormal)

logi <- fitdist(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg, "logis")
summary(logi)

# Compare the different distributions
gofstat(list(normal, exponential, gamma, lnormal, logi), 
        fitnames=c("normal", "exponential", "gamma", "lnormal", "logis"))

# conclusion: lnormal

###### Generalized linear mixed models

lmmx <- glmmTMB(shoot_biomass_seedling_mg ~ treatment*genotype + (1|plate_id), 
                 data=data_control, na.action=na.omit,
                 family = lognormal())

plot(resid(lmmx))
plot(simulateResiduals(lmmx))

# Res Identification for batch
res <-simulateResiduals(lmmx, plot = T) # check for overdispersion
testResiduals(res) # tests distribution, dispersion and outliers, testUniformity() - tests if the overall distribution conforms to expectations
testDispersion(res) # tests under and overdispersion (big spread in data, red bar is way off of distribution of data (res))
testZeroInflation(res)
testOutliers(res, type = 'bootstrap')

# Results

summary(lmmx) # final model 

## Anova summary
Anova(lmmx,type="III")

## Post-hoc / pairwise comparisons
emmeans(lmmx, pairwise ~ treatment*genotype, adjust="tukey") 
# adjust="tukey" or adjust="none" if you want the unadjusted P-values

###
#Plot

tiff("SLI1_200mM_primary root length.tiff", width= 350, height= 600)
boxplot(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg ~ x$acc, ylim=c(0, 6.5), xlab= NA, ylab= "primary root length (cm)")

boundaries <- boxplot(data_control$shoot_biomass_seedling_mg ~ x$acc, ylim=c(0, 6.5), xlab= NA, ylab= "primary root length (cm)")

text( 
  x=c(1:3), 
  y=boundaries$stats[nrow(boundaries$stats),] + 0.5, 
  paste(c("a", "a", "b"))  
)
dev.off()

```

#Statistics - GLM


##total biomass
### Col-0


```{r}
# col-0 in 1/2MS

data_C_col0 <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(genotype == "col0")

# Data distribution

aovx <- aov(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ data_C_col0$treatment)
shapiro.test(aovx$residuals)
# normal

hist(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

boxplot(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

# Visualize data
plotdist(log(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg))

# Test the different distributions
normal <- fitdist(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "norm")
summary(normal)

gamma <- fitdist(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "gamma") 
summary(gamma)

poisson <- fitdist(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "pois")
summary(poisson)

exponential <- fitdist(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "exp")
summary(exponential)

nonbinom <- fitdist(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "nbinom")
summary(nonbinom)

lnormal <- fitdist(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "lnorm")
summary(lnormal)

logi <- fitdist(data_C_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "logis")
summary(logi)

# Compare the different distributions
gofstat(list(normal, exponential, gamma, lnormal, logi), 
        fitnames=c("normal", "exponential", "gamma", "lnormal", "logis"))

# conclusion: lnormal

###### Generalized linear mixed models

lmmx <- glmmTMB(total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ treatment + (1|plate_id), 
                 data=data_C_col0, na.action=na.omit,
                 family = lognormal())

plot(resid(lmmx))
plot(simulateResiduals(lmmx)) #DHARMa: qgam was unable to calculate quantile regression for quantile 0.25. Possibly to few (unique) data points / predictions. The quantile will be ommited in plots and significance calculations. 

# Res Identification for batch
res <-simulateResiduals(lmmx, plot = T) # check for overdispersion
testResiduals(res) # tests distribution, dispersion and outliers, testUniformity() - tests if the overall distribution conforms to expectations
testDispersion(res) # tests under and overdispersion (big spread in data, red bar is way off of distribution of data (res))
testZeroInflation(res)
testOutliers(res, type = 'bootstrap')

# Results

summary(lmmx) # final model 

## Anova summary
glmm_table <- Anova(lmmx,type="II")

# Convert to dataframe and add factor names
anova_df <- as.data.frame(glmm_table)
anova_df$Factor <- rownames(anova_df)

# Round Chi-sq and p-value columns
anova_df$Chisq <- round(anova_df$Chisq, 2)
anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` <- round(anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`, 3)

# Add significance codes
anova_df$Significance <- cut(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, Inf),
  labels = c("***", "**", "*", ".", " "),
  right = FALSE
)

# Reorder columns: Factor first
anova_df <- anova_df[, c("Factor", "Chisq", "Df", "Pr(>Chisq)", "Significance")]

# Create flextable (no need for p_numeric in conditional formatting)
anova_ft <- flextable(anova_df) |> 
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor",
    Chisq = "Chi²",
    Df = "DF",
    `Pr(>Chisq)` = "p-value"
  ) |> 
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(as.character(`Pr(>Chisq)`)) < 0.05, part = "body")

# Export stats table to Word document
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table X. Type II ANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(anova_ft)

# Save to Word document

print(doc, target = "AnovaII_FW_Col0_control.docx")



# col-0 in sobitol

data_S_col0 <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(genotype == "col0")

# Data distribution

aovx <- aov(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ data_S_col0$treatment)
shapiro.test(aovx$residuals)
# normaal

hist(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

boxplot(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

# Visualize data
plotdist(log(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg))

# Test the different distributions
normal <- fitdist(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "norm")
summary(normal)

gamma <- fitdist(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "gamma") 
summary(gamma)

poisson <- fitdist(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "pois")
summary(poisson)

exponential <- fitdist(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "exp")
summary(exponential)

nonbinom <- fitdist(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "nbinom")
summary(nonbinom)

lnormal <- fitdist(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "lnorm")
summary(lnormal)

logi <- fitdist(data_S_col0$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "logis")
summary(logi)

# Compare the different distributions
gofstat(list(normal, exponential, gamma, lnormal, logi), 
        fitnames=c("normal", "exponential", "gamma", "lnormal", "logis"))

# conclusion: normal

###### Generalized linear mixed models

lmmx <- glmmTMB(log(total_biomass_seedling_mg) ~ treatment + (1|plate_id), 
                 data=data_S_col0, na.action=na.omit,
                 family = gaussian())

plot(resid(lmmx))
plot(simulateResiduals(lmmx)) #DHARMa: qgam was unable to calculate quantile regression for quantile 0.25. Possibly to few (unique) data points / predictions. The quantile will be ommited in plots and significance calculations. 

# Res Identification for batch
res <-simulateResiduals(lmmx, plot = T) # check for overdispersion
testResiduals(res) # tests distribution, dispersion and outliers, testUniformity() - tests if the overall distribution conforms to expectations
testDispersion(res) # tests under and overdispersion (big spread in data, red bar is way off of distribution of data (res))
testZeroInflation(res)
testOutliers(res, type = 'bootstrap')

# Results

summary(lmmx) # final model 

## Anova summary
glmm_table <- Anova(lmmx,type="II")

# Convert to dataframe and add factor names
anova_df <- as.data.frame(glmm_table)
anova_df$Factor <- rownames(anova_df)

# Round Chi-sq and p-value columns
anova_df$Chisq <- round(anova_df$Chisq, 2)
anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` <- round(anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`, 3)

# Add significance codes
anova_df$Significance <- cut(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, Inf),
  labels = c("***", "**", "*", ".", " "),
  right = FALSE
)

# Reorder columns: Factor first
anova_df <- anova_df[, c("Factor", "Chisq", "Df", "Pr(>Chisq)", "Significance")]

# Create flextable (no need for p_numeric in conditional formatting)
anova_ft <- flextable(anova_df) |> 
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor",
    Chisq = "Chi²",
    Df = "DF",
    `Pr(>Chisq)` = "p-value"
  ) |> 
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(as.character(`Pr(>Chisq)`)) < 0.05, part = "body")

# Export stats table to Word document
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table X. Type II ANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(anova_ft)

# Save to Word document

print(doc, target = "AnovaII_FW_Col0_sorbitol.docx")

```

###Bcat-4

```{r}
# bcat-4 in 1/2MS

data_C_bcat4 <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(genotype == "bcat4")

# Data distribution

aovx <- aov(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ data_C_bcat4$treatment)
shapiro.test(aovx$residuals)
#normaal

hist(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

boxplot(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

# Visualize data
plotdist(log(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg))

# Test the different distributions
normal <- fitdist(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "norm")
summary(normal)

gamma <- fitdist(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "gamma") 
summary(gamma)

poisson <- fitdist(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "pois")
summary(poisson)

exponential <- fitdist(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "exp")
summary(exponential)

nonbinom <- fitdist(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "nbinom")
summary(nonbinom)

lnormal <- fitdist(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "lnorm")
summary(lnormal)

logi <- fitdist(data_C_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "logis")
summary(logi)

# Compare the different distributions
gofstat(list(normal, exponential, gamma, lnormal, logi), 
        fitnames=c("normal", "exponential", "gamma", "lnormal", "logis"))

# conclusion: lnormal

###### Generalized linear mixed models

lmmx <- glmmTMB(total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ treatment + (1|plate_id), 
                 data=data_C_bcat4, na.action=na.omit,
                 family = lognormal())

plot(resid(lmmx))
plot(simulateResiduals(lmmx)) #DHARMa: qgam was unable to calculate quantile regression for quantile 0.25. Possibly to few (unique) data points / predictions. The quantile will be ommited in plots and significance calculations. 

# Res Identification for batch
res <-simulateResiduals(lmmx, plot = T) # check for overdispersion
testResiduals(res) # tests distribution, dispersion and outliers, testUniformity() - tests if the overall distribution conforms to expectations
testDispersion(res) # tests under and overdispersion (big spread in data, red bar is way off of distribution of data (res))
testZeroInflation(res)
testOutliers(res, type = 'bootstrap')

# Results

summary(lmmx) # final model 

## Anova summary
glmm_table <- Anova(lmmx,type="II")

# Convert to dataframe and add factor names
anova_df <- as.data.frame(glmm_table)
anova_df$Factor <- rownames(anova_df)

# Round Chi-sq and p-value columns
anova_df$Chisq <- round(anova_df$Chisq, 2)
anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` <- round(anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`, 3)

# Add significance codes
anova_df$Significance <- cut(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, Inf),
  labels = c("***", "**", "*", ".", " "),
  right = FALSE
)

# Reorder columns: Factor first
anova_df <- anova_df[, c("Factor", "Chisq", "Df", "Pr(>Chisq)", "Significance")]

# Create flextable (no need for p_numeric in conditional formatting)
anova_ft <- flextable(anova_df) |> 
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor",
    Chisq = "Chi²",
    Df = "DF",
    `Pr(>Chisq)` = "p-value"
  ) |> 
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(as.character(`Pr(>Chisq)`)) < 0.05, part = "body")

# Export stats table to Word document
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table X. Type II ANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(anova_ft)

# Save to Word document

print(doc, target = "AnovaII_FW_Bcat4_control.docx")


# bcat-4 in sobitol

data_S_bcat4 <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(genotype == "bcat4")

# Data distribution

aovx <- aov(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ data_S_bcat4$treatment)
shapiro.test(aovx$residuals)
# normaal

hist(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

boxplot(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

# Visualize data
plotdist(log(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg))

# Test the different distributions
normal <- fitdist(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "norm")
summary(normal)

gamma <- fitdist(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "gamma") 
summary(gamma)

poisson <- fitdist(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "pois")
summary(poisson)

exponential <- fitdist(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "exp")
summary(exponential)

nonbinom <- fitdist(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "nbinom")
summary(nonbinom)

lnormal <- fitdist(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "lnorm")
summary(lnormal)

logi <- fitdist(data_S_bcat4$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "logis")
summary(logi)

# Compare the different distributions
gofstat(list(normal, exponential, gamma, lnormal, logi), 
        fitnames=c("normal", "exponential", "gamma", "lnormal", "logis"))

# conclusion: logis

###### Generalized linear mixed models

lmmx <- glmmTMB(total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ treatment + (1|plate_id), 
                 data = data_S_bcat4, na.action = na.omit,
                 family = gaussian(link = "identity"))  # Gaussian as an approximation 

plot(resid(lmmx))
plot(simulateResiduals(lmmx)) #DHARMa: qgam was unable to calculate quantile regression for quantile 0.25. Possibly to few (unique) data points / predictions. The quantile will be ommited in plots and significance calculations. 

# Res Identification for batch
res <-simulateResiduals(lmmx, plot = T) # check for overdispersion
testResiduals(res) # tests distribution, dispersion and outliers, testUniformity() - tests if the overall distribution conforms to expectations
testDispersion(res) # tests under and overdispersion (big spread in data, red bar is way off of distribution of data (res))
testZeroInflation(res)
testOutliers(res, type = 'bootstrap')

# Results

summary(lmmx) # final model 

## Anova summary
glmm_table <- Anova(lmmx,type="II")

# Convert to dataframe and add factor names
anova_df <- as.data.frame(glmm_table)
anova_df$Factor <- rownames(anova_df)

# Round Chi-sq and p-value columns
anova_df$Chisq <- round(anova_df$Chisq, 2)
anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` <- ifelse(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` < 0.001,
  "<0.001",
  formatC(anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`, format = "f", digits = 3)
)


# Add significance codes
anova_df$Significance <- cut(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, Inf),
  labels = c("***", "**", "*", ".", " "),
  right = FALSE
)

# Reorder columns: Factor first
anova_df <- anova_df[, c("Factor", "Chisq", "Df", "Pr(>Chisq)","Significance")]

# Create flextable (no need for p_numeric in conditional formatting)
anova_ft <- flextable(anova_df) |> 
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor",
    Chisq = "Chi²",
    Df = "DF",
    `Pr(>Chisq)` = "p-value"
  ) |> 
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(as.character(`Pr(>Chisq)`)) < 0.05, part = "body")

# Export stats table to Word document
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table X. Type II ANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(anova_ft)

# Save to Word document

print(doc, target = "AnovaII_FW_Bcat4_sorbitol.docx")
```

###snrk2.2/2.3

```{r}
# snrk2.2/2.3. in 1/2MS

data_C_snrk <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(genotype == "snrk2.2/2.3")

# Data distribution

aovx <- aov(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ data_C_snrk$treatment)
shapiro.test(aovx$residuals)
# normaal

hist(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

boxplot(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

# Visualize data
plotdist(log(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg))

# Test the different distributions
normal <- fitdist(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "norm")
summary(normal)

gamma <- fitdist(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "gamma") 
summary(gamma)

poisson <- fitdist(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "pois")
summary(poisson)

exponential <- fitdist(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "exp")
summary(exponential)

nonbinom <- fitdist(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "nbinom")
summary(nonbinom)

lnormal <- fitdist(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "lnorm")
summary(lnormal)

logi <- fitdist(data_C_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "logis")
summary(logi)

# Compare the different distributions
gofstat(list(normal, exponential, gamma, lnormal, logi), 
        fitnames=c("normal", "exponential", "gamma", "lnormal", "logis"))

# conclusion: gamma

###### Generalized linear mixed models

lmmx <- glmmTMB(total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ treatment + (1|plate_id), 
                 data=data_C_snrk, na.action=na.omit,
                 family = Gamma(link = "log"))

plot(resid(lmmx))
plot(simulateResiduals(lmmx)) #DHARMa: qgam was unable to calculate quantile regression for quantile 0.25. Possibly to few (unique) data points / predictions. The quantile will be ommited in plots and significance calculations. 

# Res Identification for batch
res <-simulateResiduals(lmmx, plot = T) # check for overdispersion
testResiduals(res) # tests distribution, dispersion and outliers, testUniformity() - tests if the overall distribution conforms to expectations
testDispersion(res) # tests under and overdispersion (big spread in data, red bar is way off of distribution of data (res))
testZeroInflation(res)
testOutliers(res, type = 'bootstrap')

# Results

summary(lmmx) # final model 

## Anova summary
glmm_table <- Anova(lmmx,type="II")

# Convert to dataframe and add factor names
anova_df <- as.data.frame(glmm_table)
anova_df$Factor <- rownames(anova_df)

# Round Chi-sq and p-value columns
anova_df$Chisq <- round(anova_df$Chisq, 2)
anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` <- ifelse(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` < 0.001,
  "<0.001",
  formatC(anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`, format = "f", digits = 3)
)

# Add significance codes
anova_df$Significance <- cut(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, Inf),
  labels = c("***", "**", "*", ".", " "),
  right = FALSE
)

# Reorder columns: Factor first
anova_df <- anova_df[, c("Factor", "Chisq", "Df", "Pr(>Chisq)")]

# Create flextable (no need for p_numeric in conditional formatting)
anova_ft <- flextable(anova_df) |> 
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor",
    Chisq = "Chi²",
    Df = "DF",
    `Pr(>Chisq)` = "p-value"
  ) |> 
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(as.character(`Pr(>Chisq)`)) < 0.05, part = "body")

# Export stats table to Word document
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table X. Type II ANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(anova_ft)

# Save to Word document

print(doc, target = "AnovaII_FW_snrk_control.docx")

# snrk2.2/2.3. in sobitol

data_S_snrk <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(genotype == "snrk2.2/2.3")

# Data distribution

aovx <- aov(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ data_S_snrk$treatment)
shapiro.test(aovx$residuals)
# normaal

hist(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

boxplot(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

# Visualize data
plotdist(log(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg))

# Test the different distributions
normal <- fitdist(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "norm")
summary(normal)

gamma <- fitdist(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "gamma") 
summary(gamma)

poisson <- fitdist(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "pois")
summary(poisson)

exponential <- fitdist(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "exp")
summary(exponential)

nonbinom <- fitdist(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "nbinom")
summary(nonbinom)

lnormal <- fitdist(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "lnorm")
summary(lnormal)

logi <- fitdist(data_S_snrk$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "logis")
summary(logi)

# Compare the different distributions
gofstat(list(normal, exponential, gamma, lnormal, logi), 
        fitnames=c("normal", "exponential", "gamma", "lnormal", "logis"))

# conclusion: normal

###### Generalized linear mixed models

lmmx <- glmmTMB(total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ treatment + (1|plate_id), 
                 data = data_S_snrk, na.action = na.omit,
                 family = gaussian())  # Gaussian as an approximation

plot(resid(lmmx))
plot(simulateResiduals(lmmx)) #DHARMa: qgam was unable to calculate quantile regression for quantile 0.25. Possibly to few (unique) data points / predictions. The quantile will be ommited in plots and significance calculations. 

# Res Identification for batch
res <-simulateResiduals(lmmx, plot = T) # check for overdispersion
testResiduals(res) # tests distribution, dispersion and outliers, testUniformity() - tests if the overall distribution conforms to expectations
testDispersion(res) # tests under and overdispersion (big spread in data, red bar is way off of distribution of data (res))
testZeroInflation(res)
testOutliers(res, type = 'bootstrap')

# Results

summary(lmmx) # final model 

## Anova summary
glmm_table <- Anova(lmmx,type="II")

# Convert to dataframe and add factor names
anova_df <- as.data.frame(glmm_table)
anova_df$Factor <- rownames(anova_df)

# Round Chi-sq and p-value columns
anova_df$Chisq <- round(anova_df$Chisq, 2)
anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` <- ifelse(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` < 0.001,
  "<0.001",
  formatC(anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`, format = "f", digits = 3)
)

# Add significance codes
anova_df$Significance <- cut(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, Inf),
  labels = c("***", "**", "*", ".", " "),
  right = FALSE
)

# Reorder columns: Factor first
anova_df <- anova_df[, c("Factor", "Chisq", "Df", "Pr(>Chisq)", "Significance")]

# Create flextable (no need for p_numeric in conditional formatting)
anova_ft <- flextable(anova_df) |> 
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor",
    Chisq = "Chi²",
    Df = "DF",
    `Pr(>Chisq)` = "p-value"
  ) |> 
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(as.character(`Pr(>Chisq)`)) < 0.05, part = "body")

# Export stats table to Word document
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table X. Type II ANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(anova_ft)

# Save to Word document

print(doc, target = "AnovaII_FW_snrk_sorbitol.docx")
```

###sweet11/12

```{r}
# sweet11/12 in 1/2MS

data_C_sweet <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "control") %>%
  filter(genotype == "sweet11/12")

# Data distribution

aovx <- aov(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ data_C_sweet$treatment)
shapiro.test(aovx$residuals)
# normaal

hist(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

boxplot(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

# Visualize data
plotdist(log(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg))

# Test the different distributions
normal <- fitdist(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "norm")
summary(normal)

gamma <- fitdist(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "gamma") 
summary(gamma)

poisson <- fitdist(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "pois")
summary(poisson)

exponential <- fitdist(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "exp")
summary(exponential)

nonbinom <- fitdist(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "nbinom")
summary(nonbinom)

lnormal <- fitdist(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "lnorm")
summary(lnormal)

logi <- fitdist(data_C_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "logis")
summary(logi)

# Compare the different distributions
gofstat(list(normal, exponential, gamma, lnormal, logi), 
        fitnames=c("normal", "exponential", "gamma", "lnormal", "logis"))

# conclusion: normal

###### Generalized linear mixed models

lmmx <- glmmTMB(total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ treatment + (1|plate_id), 
                 data=data_C_sweet, na.action=na.omit,
                 family = gaussian())

plot(resid(lmmx))
plot(simulateResiduals(lmmx)) #DHARMa: qgam was unable to calculate quantile regression for quantile 0.25. Possibly to few (unique) data points / predictions. The quantile will be ommited in plots and significance calculations. 

# Res Identification for batch
res <-simulateResiduals(lmmx, plot = T) # check for overdispersion
testResiduals(res) # tests distribution, dispersion and outliers, testUniformity() - tests if the overall distribution conforms to expectations
testDispersion(res) # tests under and overdispersion (big spread in data, red bar is way off of distribution of data (res))
testZeroInflation(res)
testOutliers(res, type = 'bootstrap')

# Results

summary(lmmx) # final model 

## Anova summary
glmm_table <- Anova(lmmx,type="II")

# Convert to dataframe and add factor names
anova_df <- as.data.frame(glmm_table)
anova_df$Factor <- rownames(anova_df)

# Round Chi-sq and p-value columns
anova_df$Chisq <- round(anova_df$Chisq, 2)
anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` <- ifelse(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` < 0.001,
  "<0.001",
  formatC(anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`, format = "f", digits = 3)
)

# Add significance codes
anova_df$Significance <- cut(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, Inf),
  labels = c("***", "**", "*", ".", " "),
  right = FALSE
)

# Reorder columns: Factor first
anova_df <- anova_df[, c("Factor", "Chisq", "Df", "Pr(>Chisq)", "Significance")]

# Create flextable (no need for p_numeric in conditional formatting)
anova_ft <- flextable(anova_df) |> 
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor",
    Chisq = "Chi²",
    Df = "DF",
    `Pr(>Chisq)` = "p-value"
  ) |> 
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(as.character(`Pr(>Chisq)`)) < 0.05, part = "body")

# Export stats table to Word document
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table X. Type II ANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(anova_ft)

# Save to Word document

print(doc, target = "AnovaII_FW_sweet_control.docx")

# sweet11/12 in sobitol

data_S_sweet <- biomass %>%
  filter(media == "sorbitol") %>%
  filter(genotype == "sweet11/12")

# Data distribution

aovx <- aov(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ data_S_sweet$treatment)
shapiro.test(aovx$residuals)
# normaal

hist(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

boxplot(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg)

# Visualize data
plotdist(log(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg))

# Test the different distributions
normal <- fitdist(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "norm")
summary(normal)

gamma <- fitdist(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "gamma") 
summary(gamma)

poisson <- fitdist(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "pois")
summary(poisson)

exponential <- fitdist(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "exp")
summary(exponential)

nonbinom <- fitdist(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "nbinom")
summary(nonbinom)

lnormal <- fitdist(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "lnorm")
summary(lnormal)

logi <- fitdist(data_S_sweet$total_biomass_seedling_mg, "logis")
summary(logi)

# Compare the different distributions
gofstat(list(normal, exponential, gamma, lnormal, logi), 
        fitnames=c("normal", "exponential", "gamma", "lnormal", "logis"))

# conclusion:  gamma

###### Generalized linear mixed models

lmmx <- glmmTMB(total_biomass_seedling_mg ~ treatment + (1|plate_id), 
                 data = data_S_sweet, na.action = na.omit,
                 family =  Gamma(link = "log"))

plot(resid(lmmx))
plot(simulateResiduals(lmmx)) #DHARMa: qgam was unable to calculate quantile regression for quantile 0.25. Possibly to few (unique) data points / predictions. The quantile will be ommited in plots and significance calculations. 

# Res Identification for batch
res <-simulateResiduals(lmmx, plot = T) # check for overdispersion
testResiduals(res) # tests distribution, dispersion and outliers, testUniformity() - tests if the overall distribution conforms to expectations
testDispersion(res) # tests under and overdispersion (big spread in data, red bar is way off of distribution of data (res))
testZeroInflation(res)
testOutliers(res, type = 'bootstrap')

# Results

summary(lmmx) # final model 

## Anova summary
glmm_table <- Anova(lmmx,type="II")

# Convert to dataframe and add factor names
anova_df <- as.data.frame(glmm_table)
anova_df$Factor <- rownames(anova_df)

# Round Chi-sq and p-value columns
anova_df$Chisq <- round(anova_df$Chisq, 2)
anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` <- ifelse(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)` < 0.001,
  "<0.001",
  formatC(anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`, format = "f", digits = 3)
)

# Add significance codes
anova_df$Significance <- cut(
  anova_df$`Pr(>Chisq)`,
  breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, Inf),
  labels = c("***", "**", "*", ".", " "),
  right = FALSE
)

# Reorder columns: Factor first
anova_df <- anova_df[, c("Factor", "Chisq", "Df", "Pr(>Chisq)", "Significance")]

# Create flextable (no need for p_numeric in conditional formatting)
anova_ft <- flextable(anova_df) |> 
  set_header_labels(
    Factor = "Factor",
    Chisq = "Chi²",
    Df = "DF",
    `Pr(>Chisq)` = "p-value"
  ) |> 
  autofit() |>
  theme_booktabs() |>
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(as.character(`Pr(>Chisq)`)) < 0.05, part = "body")

# Export stats table to Word document
doc <- read_docx() |>
  body_add_par("Table X. Type II ANOVA Results", style = "heading 2") |>
  body_add_flextable(anova_ft)

# Save to Word document

print(doc, target = "AnovaII_FW_sweet_sorbitol.docx")
```
